message:
fix(cleanup): fail-closed activity probes and precise stale release reporting

Fix a safety issue where truncated filesystem activity probes (git
ls-files or walkdir hitting ACTIVITY_PROBE_PATH_LIMIT) were silently
returning false, causing the cleanup loop to incorrectly release
reservations that might still be actively held. Truncated scans now
return None, and callers unwrap_or(true) to fail closed -- keeping the
reservation alive when probe results are inconclusive.

Refactor the probe functions:
- check_git_listed_activity: early-return on activity hit, distinguish
  between probe-limit truncation (None) and clean git exit (Some)
- check_directory_activity_fallback: return Option<bool> instead of bool
- Extract check_glob_activity_fallback as a standalone function

Switch from release_reservations_by_ids (returns count) to
release_reservations_by_ids_returning_ids (returns exact ID list) so the
stale-release detector can report precisely which reservations were
actually released vs. already-released or skipped.

Tests:
- check_filesystem_activity falls back when git glob probe fails
- check_filesystem_activity falls back when git directory probe fails
- glob probe limit hits fail closed
- directory probe limit hits fail closed

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
